text: Continuous integration is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time new code is added, automated builds and tests are triggered. This means the software is compiled, and a series of tests are run to check for errors. The main benefit is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. CI pipelines are used to manage this process, automatically running both unit and integration tests. Because of this, teams can identify and fix bugs quickly, reduce conflicts between developers, and maintain a more stable codebase throughout the development cycle.
